mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real short-term assets combined with stable consumer prices indicate strong household liquidity without inflationary pressure, supporting equity valuations over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to produce positive 60d returns in over 55% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during late-cycle expansions reflects robust manufacturing demand and capacity utilization, producing positive short-term S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with INDPRO momentum, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls directly correlate with rising household income and corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive 20d returns, with out-of-sample directional accuracy remaining below 52% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly inflation readings above 0.4% trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in over 65% of high-inflation trigger months, or inflation threshold loses predictive power in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and housing starts signals synchronized economic recovery across financial and real sectors, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Combined GDP and housing signal yields negative 20d returns in more than 55% of cases, or interaction effect vanishes in panel regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicates demand destruction from higher rates, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines fail to predict negative 60d returns during tightening periods, with directional accuracy dropping below 50% in out-of-sample tests.
